User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Rebuy Tracker application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player in the poker game.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

initialBuyIn.description: The initial buy-in amount for the player.

Rebuy.title: Rebuy
Rebuy.description: Represents a re-buy event for a player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rebuy entity.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the re-buy occurred.

dealerId.description: Reference to Dealer who approved the rebuy. (Relationship: Dealer 1:N Rebuy)

Dealer.title: Dealer
Dealer.description: Represents a dealer in the poker game.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Dealer entity.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Only the authenticated user can read/write their own document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores player information, linked to a specific user.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player.

definition.description: Stores dealer information, linked to a specific user.

params.name: dealerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the dealer.

definition.description: Stores rebuy information for a specific player. Only Dealers can create/delete these. Includes denormalized 'dealerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player.

params.name: rebuyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the rebuy.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Rebuy Tracker application, focusing on secure user authentication and role-based access control for managing players and rebuys. It ensures Authorization Independence, addresses QAPs, and simplifies security rules. The database structure includes separate collections for users, players, dealers and rebuy. The `users` collection stores basic user information including their roles (`Dealer` or `Player`). `players` and `dealers` collections store corresponding data for player and dealer. The `rebuys` collection store information for each rebuy. For authorization independence, each `Rebuy` document denormalizes the `dealerId` to explicitly indicate which dealer approved the rebuy, which facilitates Dealer role-based access control. Additionally, authorization is simplified by storing `Rebuy` documents under the `/players/{playerId}/rebuys/{rebuyId}` path, establishing ownership and simplifying rules based on the logged-in user's UID.
